page: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture, utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48,570.2 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,654.0. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 955.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.36 GB/sec. However, memory subsystem performance is strong in uncached read-only large block operations, reaching 178.7 GB/sec, while cached small block reads average 1,893.6 GB/sec. Database operations and static web serving throughput perform in the average tier, with Redis SET operations achieving 36.99 million operations per second and static web serving reaching 32.53 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based Graviton architecture offers a balanced resource density of vCPU to memory, making it a viable alternative to x86 instances for workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. The instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations. It is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference, where its performance falls into the weaker percentiles.
description: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average multi-core performance, highlighted by a multi-core Geekbench score of 11,654.0 and strong uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 178.7 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core performance is weaker, with a Geekbench single-core score of 955.0. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads, making it suitable for applications such as software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, while being less optimal for single-threaded processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated ARMv8 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is an ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network speed.
tagline: A balanced ARM-based instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARMv8 Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio.
Strong uncached memory read bandwidth of 178.7 GB/sec.
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU.
Well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and database operations.
